prompt: Translate the following English sentence into French, Japanese, and Arabic, and add a brief note on any phrase that was tricky to render faithfully: "The silence between us said more than any argument could have."
response:
Here is the sentence translated into French, Japanese, and Arabic, along with notes on any tricky phrases:

French: 
Le silence entre nous en disait plus que n'importe quelle dispute n'aurait pu le faire.
Note: The phrase "more than any argument could have" was tricky to render faithfully in French. A literal translation would be "plus que n'importe quel argument n'aurait pu le faire", but this sounds awkward in French. Instead, I used "dispute" (dispute/argument) which flows better.

Japanese:
私たちの間の沈黙は、どんな議論よりも多くを語っていた。
Watashitachi no aida no chinmoku wa, donna giron yori mo ōku o katatte ita.
Note: The phrase "more than any argument could have" was challenging to translate directly into Japanese. Instead, I used the phrase "どんな議論よりも多くを語っていた" which means "spoke more than any argument". This captures the meaning that the silence conveyed more than words could.

Arabic:
الصمت بيننا قال أكثر مما قد يفعل أي جدال.
Alssamt baymana qal 'akthar mimma qad yafaul 'ayy jadal.
Note: The phrase "more than any argument could have" was tricky to render faithfully in Arabic. I used "أكثر مما قد يفعل أي جدال" which literally means "more than what any argument may do". This captures the intended meaning that the silence conveyed more than any argument could.
